Ktor.summary_abuse: Ktor is a server-side and client-side Kotlin framework. It has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal.

Kubernetes Dashboard.history: Kubernetes Dashboard was the official general-purpose web UI for Kubernetes clusters, part of the broader Kubernetes project maintained under the CNCF. The project has been archived and officially deprecated, with the community moving toward alternative tools like Headlamp.
Kubernetes Dashboard.summary_abuse: Kubernetes Dashboard is a cluster administration tool deployed in private infrastructure. It has no consumer-facing surface and no notable abuse signal, though misconfigured instances have historically been exploited by attackers for cryptomining when exposed to the internet.

Kubio Builder.history: Kubio Builder is a WordPress Gutenberg page builder plugin developed by ExtendThemes. It was launched around 2021 with a free version on WordPress.org and a premium tier with advanced features.
Kubio Builder.summary_abuse: Kubio Builder is a self-hosted WordPress plugin. It has no consumer-facing hosted surface and no documented abuse signal.

Kudobuzz.history: Kudobuzz was founded in 2013 in Accra, Ghana, emerging from the Meltwater Entrepreneurial School of Technology (MEST). It helps SMEs collect and display customer reviews from social platforms, with backing from 500 Global.
Kudobuzz.summary_abuse: Kudobuzz aggregates customer reviews for e-commerce stores. It has no notable consumer-facing surface for fraud. Fake review generation is a theoretical risk for any review platform but no specific incidents have been documented for Kudobuzz.

Kueez.history: Founded in 2015 in Israel by Ori Mendi, Shahaf Kimhi, and Daniel Tony, Kueez is an entertainment platform offering quizzes and short-form games designed for high engagement and social sharing.
Kueez.summary_abuse: Kueez is an ad-supported quiz and entertainment platform. Such platforms commonly carry heavy tracker density and can be vectors for misleading or clickbait advertising. The free consumer model is ad-driven, which increases exposure to low-quality ad networks.

KueskiPay.history: Kueski was founded in 2012 in Mexico to provide personal loans to underbanked consumers. In 2021 it launched Kueski Pay, a buy now pay later product, becoming Mexico's leading BNPL provider with partnerships including Amazon Mexico.
KueskiPay.summary_abuse: KueskiPay is a BNPL payment method primarily used in Mexico. As a financial product, it processes consumer lending data. BNPL products generally face some risk of fraud from synthetic identity attacks but KueskiPay uses AI-based credit scoring. No specific abuse incidents documented.

KUKUI.history: KUKUI was founded in 2011 in San Jose, CA. Provides all-in-one marketing platform for auto repair shops including website, CRM, and POS integrations. Raised $28.6M from SSM Partners. Serves 2,000+ auto repair shops. Ranked #76 on Inc. 500 fastest-growing list.
KUKUI.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Niche automotive marketing platform. Collects customer data for auto repair shops. Limited public security information.

Kulea Marketing.history: Founded in 2015 by marketers in Buckingham, England (based at Bletchley Park), Kulea is a marketing automation platform targeting digital agencies and SMEs, with first commercial sales in 2018.
Kulea Marketing.summary_abuse: Kulea Marketing is a B2B marketing automation platform. It has no notable consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Kundo.history: Founded in 2009 in Stockholm, Sweden by Bjorn Lilja, Emil Stenstrom, and Jonatan Larsson, Kundo is a customer service platform for Nordic markets. It has invested in Cloud Capital and expanded to an AI-first service experience platform.
Kundo.summary_abuse: Kundo is a B2B customer service platform primarily serving Nordic companies. It has no notable consumer-facing abuse risk and no documented incidents.

Kuroco.history: Kuroco is a headless CMS built and maintained by Diverta Inc., a Japanese CMS company founded in 2005. Kuroco's official version was released in April 2021, evolving from Diverta's earlier RCMS product that has been in development for over 20 years.
Kuroco.summary_abuse: Kuroco is a headless CMS for enterprise publishers. It has no consumer-facing hosted content surface and no documented abuse signal.

KuronekoServer CDN.history: KuronekoServer CDN is operated by a volunteer student organization in Japan since 2018. It provides free CDN, mirror hosting, and image delivery services primarily for VRChat users.
KuronekoServer CDN.summary_abuse: KuronekoServer CDN is a free CDN run by Japanese student volunteers. Free file hosting services with open upload can be used for malware distribution or phishing kit hosting, but this service is very small and community-oriented with no documented abuse cases.

Kustomer.history: Kustomer was founded in 2015 in New York by Brad Birnbaum and Jeremy Suriel. Meta (Facebook) acquired it for approximately $1 billion in February 2022. In May 2023, Meta spun Kustomer out at a $250M valuation with original investors reinvesting $60M.
Kustomer.summary_abuse: Kustomer is a B2B CRM and customer service platform. It handles customer interaction data but has no consumer-facing surface that is commonly exploited for fraud. No notable abuse incidents documented.

KVCore.history: kvCORE (originally Kunversion) was rebranded and launched in 2017 by Inside Real Estate. Used by 250,000+ real estate agents and 190,000+ paid users with 470+ MLS integrations. In mid-2024 Inside Real Estate rebranded kvCORE as BoldTrail with enhanced AI features.
KVCore.summary_abuse: No major abuse incidents. Real estate CRM collecting client lead data. Rebranded to BoldTrail in 2024. Some user complaints about AI lead generation calling being overly aggressive.

Kwai pixel.history: Kwai pixel is the advertising conversion tracking pixel for Kwai, the international brand of Kuaishou, a Chinese short-video platform co-founded by Su Hua and Cheng Yixiao in 2011. Kuaishou Technology went public on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange in February 2021. The pixel allows advertisers to track website events and conversions attributed to Kwai ads.
Kwai pixel.summary_abuse: Kwai pixel is an advertising tracker that collects behavioral and conversion data from site visitors and transmits it to Kuaishou servers in China. This creates cross-border data transfer concerns for users in the EU and other jurisdictions due to Chinese data laws (PIPL, DSL) that allow government access to data stored in China. Privacy risk is elevated compared to Western ad platforms due to jurisdictional exposure.

Kwanko.history: Kwanko was founded in 2003 as a French affiliate marketing and performance marketing network. The company expanded across Europe and internationally over the following years. In 2022, Kwanko merged with verticalADS Group, becoming part of a larger digital advertising holding. Kwanko offers affiliate, display, email, and mobile performance marketing services.
Kwanko.summary_abuse: As an affiliate marketing network, Kwanko is involved in performance-based advertising which can occasionally be exploited by affiliates promoting low-quality or deceptive products. Affiliate networks are known to have mixed publisher quality and have been used for adware or pop-up campaigns. Kwanko operates under French and EU regulations including GDPR, which provides some governance. No specific public incidents documented.

Kwanzoo.history: Kwanzoo is a US-based account-based marketing and GTM automation platform founded around 2015. The company positions itself as an AI-powered ABM solution helping B2B companies identify and engage target accounts. It has remained a small independent vendor in the competitive ABM/intent data space alongside larger players like Demandbase and 6sense.
Kwanzoo.summary_abuse: Kwanzoo is a B2B marketing data platform that processes third-party intent signals and account-level behavioral data to identify potential buyers. As with most ABM platforms, it collects and utilizes visitor data across websites using tracking technologies. Privacy risk is moderate for a B2B tool. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Kwipped.history: Kwipped was founded in 2014 in North Carolina, USA, as a B2B online marketplace connecting businesses with equipment rental and leasing providers. The platform allows businesses to post equipment needs and receive quotes from multiple suppliers, covering categories like medical, scientific, industrial, and AV equipment. It has remained a niche US-focused marketplace.
Kwipped.summary_abuse: Kwipped is a B2B equipment rental marketplace with limited public-facing risk surface. As a business-to-business platform requiring registration, it presents low risk for consumer-facing abuse. No notable security incidents or abuse patterns documented. Standard marketplace risks like fraudulent listings or non-delivery apply but are not reported as prominent issues.

KYKLO.history: KYKLO was founded in 2015 in Buffalo, NY. Provides B2B ecommerce and product content solutions for industrial manufacturers and distributors. Has a data pool of 7M+ industrial SKUs. Clients include Schneider Electric, Festo, Mitsubishi Electric Automation. Acquired by Epicor in June 2024.
KYKLO.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Industrial B2B ecommerce platform acquired by Epicor in 2024. Reputable enterprise software company backing provides stability.

Kyvio.history: Kyvio was founded around 2016-2017 as an online marketing platform for creating membership sites and sales funnels. It targets online course creators, coaches, and digital marketers with an all-in-one funnel-building and membership solution. The platform competes with ClickFunnels and Kartra in the small business online marketing niche.
Kyvio.summary_abuse: Kyvio is used by online marketers and course creators to build sales funnels and membership sites. The platform can be used to host aggressive marketing funnels with upsell stacking and urgency tactics common in online marketing. Funnel-builder platforms attract a mix of legitimate and questionable marketers. No specific major abuse incidents documented for Kyvio specifically.
